mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 9.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts household consumption and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 9.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in residential housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicate synchronized household balance sheet expansion, which amplifies aggregate demand and lifts equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of historical episodes, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter acceleration in the industrial production index reflects rising capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which expands corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns, resulting in a negative correlation over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns turn negative despite sustained INDPRO acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.5 percent per annum signals robust aggregate demand, which directly supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth remains above 2.5 percent while 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, indicating a decoupling between real output and equity pricing.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above a 2.5 percent annualized rate, it triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally despite CPI inflation persistently exceeding 2.5 percent, or the signal produces positive forward returns in more than 55% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of historical episodes, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained divergence where housing starts outpace industrial production growth indicates a shift toward domestic consumption-led growth, which reduces reliance on external demand and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST growth rate exceeds INDPRO growth rate by 0.5 percentage points
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.
